reasoning: Across 11 dims: (1) OUTLINE WEIGHT — peers use clean 1px hard outlines at their native scale; candidate has thicker compound outlines with color-tinted inner borders, heavier than peers. (2) OUTLINE COLOR — peers use near-black dark outlines consistently; candidate mixes dark outlines with hue-shifted borders (dark pink on body, dark teal on wings), partial mismatch. (3) SHADOW DIRECTION — indeterminate on peers due to small scale; candidate shows top-left light source with consistent cast shadows, no strong conflict but unverifiable. (4) SHADOW COUNT — peers at their resolution carry 1-2 shading steps max; candidate has 3+ distinct value steps per region (wing gradients, skin, petal layers) — substantially more complex. (5) HIGHLIGHT INTENSITY — peers show restrained or no specular highlights; candidate has prominent near-white hotspots on head, wings, and flower petals — noticeably brighter than peer family. (6) COLOR TEMPERATURE — peers span a wide neutral-to-cool palette (greens, purples, grays, earth tones); candidate is warm-dominant (saturated pinks, peach) with a cool accent (cyan wings) — temperature profile diverges from peer median. (7) DETAIL DENSITY — most severe failure: peers are tightly constrained minimal sprites with ~16-32px character heights; candidate at matching scale is ~60-70px tall with elaborate flora, multiple wing layers, facial features, decorative flowers — detail density is 3-4x peer norm. (8) PROPORTION — candidate's head-to-body ratio and overall silhouette complexity far exceed the compact, readable shapes of peers; silhouette reads as 'elaborate' vs peers' 'clean icon'. (9) RENDERING STYLE — peers follow classic Denzi flat-cell pixel art; candidate uses a more contemporary 'painterly pixel' approach with gradient-simulated shading and anti-aliased curves — generation gap is visible. (10) PALETTE OVERLAP — peer family shares a recurring set of ~6-8 anchor colors; candidate's dominant pink/peach/cyan cluster appears in almost none of the peer sprites — palette island. (11) CHARM LEVEL — peers are cute in a retro minimal way (small creature charm); candidate is cute in a modern elaborate fantasy-fairy way — charm register mismatches the peer family's restrained aesthetic. Net: 5 out of 11 dims are clear failures, 3 are marginal; only shadow direction and some partial outline consistency survive scrutiny.
